overview:
The AZ-104 exam validates the skills required to implement, manage, and monitor an organization's Microsoft Azure environment. It tests a candidate's ability to manage identities, governance, storage, compute, and virtual networks, ensuring they can maintain a secure and efficient cloud infrastructure.

This exam is designed for Azure Administrators who have experience implementing and managing cloud solutions. It is a rigorous technical exam that requires a deep understanding of both the Azure Portal and command-line tools like Azure CLI and PowerShell.

Prerequisites are not mandatory, but it is highly recommended that candidates have a foundational understanding of cloud concepts, ideally achieved through the AZ-900 certification, and at least six months of hands-on experience with Azure services.
recertification_info: Recertification is free and can be completed annually via a lightweight online assessment on the Microsoft Learn platform before the current certification expires.
preparation_tips: Build a free Azure account and perform every lab manually to gain muscle memory.
Master the difference between Azure AD (Entra ID) roles and Azure RBAC roles.
Practice creating and managing Virtual Networks, including Peering and NSGs.
Understand the nuances of Azure Storage redundancy options (LRS, GRS, ZRS, RA-GRS).
Learn to deploy Virtual Machines using ARM templates and Bicep.
Study the specific differences between Azure Load Balancer and Application Gateway.
Get comfortable with Azure PowerShell and Azure CLI commands for common tasks.
Deep dive into Backup and Site Recovery configurations for disaster recovery scenarios.
Understand how to implement Azure Policy and Management Groups for governance.
Review the scaling and availability options for App Services and Virtual Machine Scale Sets.
exam_day_tips: Read the scenario-based questions carefully; a single word like 'least effort' can change the correct answer.
Manage your time strictly; if a case study is too long, mark it and move forward.
Use the process of elimination for multiple-choice questions to narrow down options.
Be prepared for 'Yes/No' series questions where you cannot go back to previous answers in that set.
Ensure your identification and system requirements are verified if taking the exam remotely.
Stay calm during the case study sections; the answers are usually embedded in the provided text.
recommended_experience: Intermediate level; 6+ months of hands-on Azure administration experience.
